evals.prompt: Build the VideoCard component for the AI Agency SaaS dashboard. It needs to show title, niche, quality score, publish status, and let users click to view details.
evals.expected_output: TypeScript React component with strict types, React.memo, full keyboard accessibility, semantic HTML, and AI Agency brand colors.
expectations: The response uses TypeScript with a typed VideoCardProps interface (no any)
The response wraps the component in React.memo
The response includes role=button, tabIndex=0, and onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space
The response uses an article element for semantic HTML
The response uses AI Agency brand colors (Navy #0A1628 or Gold #D4AF37)

evals.prompt: The SaaS dashboard is loading slowly. Lighthouse shows LCP of 5.8s and a bundle size of 2.4MB. What do we fix?
evals.expected_output: Diagnose LCP, apply React.lazy code-splitting, move data fetching to Server Components, and target LCP < 2.5s with initial JS < 500KB.
expectations: The response identifies the LCP root element and recommends preloading it
The response recommends React.lazy() + Suspense for heavy components
The response recommends moving data fetching to Next.js Server Components
The response targets LCP < 2.5s and initial bundle < 500KB

evals.prompt: An accessibility audit flagged 6 violations: no keyboard access on chart tooltips, missing form labels on dropdown, color contrast 2.8:1, modal does not trap focus, missing alt text on thumbnails, quality score bar has no text alternative.
evals.expected_output: Provide specific code fixes for all 6 WCAG 2.1 AA violations.
expectations: The response adds tabIndex=0 and onKeyDown to chart tooltips
The response adds htmlFor on label or aria-label on the dropdown
The response specifies color contrast must be at least 4.5:1 (WCAG AA)
The response implements focus trap with role=dialog and aria-modal=true
The response uses meter element or aria attributes for the quality score bar
